rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (noun) / ceremony — hard ban
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 27 'Practice is core' uses practice(n), but this is M6 (capstone): the AE101 carve-out earns practice(n) at M4 and runs fluent M5-M6. No ritual/ceremony anywhere in body. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body (lines 1-73) carries no dated markers, 'fixed' annotations, or session-biography. All date-stamps and audit history live below the maintainer cut at line 74.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Register match.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 quintet honored: Boris-direct framing ('Two runs give you gaps the three-pattern itself didn't anticipate'), Rory reframe ('The reading is the loop, not the sending'), Godin warmth ('Keep on learning and sharing'), Risto forward-optimism ('future you inherits the lesson'). M6 mood = practitioner fluency; Risto-lead permitted per mood-arc constraint on send-off/vision beats. No atmospheric/isolation imagery; no negation-callout self-contradiction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rough-cut drafts for articles.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Article-drafting rule. Target is an AE101 module file, not an article.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit original when paraphrasing named technique.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Klaassen credited inline at line 8 (Prework). Practitioner frameworks (Ronacher three-pattern, Cherny verifier shapes, compound engineering) attributed in the maintainer 'Frameworks riffed on' block, appropriate for closer attribution. Creator-name 'Antti' at line 72 is the capstone-close sign-off, in-scope-by-design per rule 6 second exception (M6 final module, personal sign-off) and documented in the maintainer block at line 77. Line 46 'our bar' = student+team collective voice (an eval asserts the team's standard), a keep-case under the author-we sub-rule; substitute test 'the training authors' bar' reads weird, confirming it is not author-we.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses second person throughout ('You walk in holding two runs', 'you shipped the second skill'). No 'the student' in body region; all third-person 'the student' usage sits below the maintainer cut. No trainer-delivery mechanics in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'eval' is earned in body at line 46 with a one-breath primer ('An eval is the automated check that says...'). 'session-shaper' and 'three-pattern' land operationally in the module's exercises (Phase 1/2) and were earned upstream in M4/M5. 'substrate' and other maintainer-only terms do not leak into the body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rescue blockquotes (lines 27, 29) state real consequences rather than reassuring a choice is fine. 'You lose a bit of value but you can still do this module's exercises' informs of actual cost; 'Despair not' is a light Godin-warmth touch, not the defensive 'no penalty / it's OK if / not a downgrade' shape the rule targets. None of the flagged reassurance phrasings appear.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: About capturing a user-given verbal frame verbatim in a reference artefact. No such frame-capture surface in this module file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body carries one student-side practitioner mention (Klaassen at line 8, Prework pre-read). All other attribution (Ronacher, Cherny, Curran, Charles) is confined to maintainer notes, which are exempt. No practitioner named multiple times in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Punchy lines ('The reading is the loop, not the sending', 'the loop closes when the lesson ships') are accurate slogans for this module's move and don't overstate a subset that needs a downstream boundary paragraph. No softened-to-hedged-middle or dropped framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project strategy docs, NOT student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No defensive-against-vendor or not-marketing framing in body. 'vendor' does not appear in the body region (only in maintainer source labels below the cut). 'agent scale' / 'agent-produced' are operational, not positioning. Body teaches; it does not defend the curriculum's posture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: This is M6 (final core module). Terms in lead-ins and LO bullets (eval, verifier, judge, gate, skill, session-shaper, three-pattern) are at or below the arc's current vocabulary level by M6 and land operationally in this module's own exercises/lectures. No future-module vocabulary planted prematurely.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit closer when trimming body-prose forcing function.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Closers ('## Human close', '## Next') land on maxim/example ('The reading is the loop, not the sending. It's yours now.') with no dangling pronouns pointing at trimmed sentences and no bypass-the-agent punchlines ('write that yourself' / 'do it manually'). The module file has no fenced prompts to trim around.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Past tense for exercise recap sections — heading and body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: This module file has no 'What happened:' / recap section. The closers ('## Human close', '## Next') are forward-looking by design. Line 48's 'Two runs tested; the diagnosis learned' describes the test-learn-encode mechanism, not a student-action recap. No recap surface to apply the tense rule to.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Human-to-agent verbs stay peer-flat: 'nudge it once', 'paste the packaged re-send', 'ask it to surface'. No bark/order/command/whip/drag/boss verbs in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Frames forward and aspirational ('future you inherits the lesson', 'so future you inherits the lesson', 'The kit, yours'). No corrective-gap framing ('you already think', 'you're missing'). No ability-tier pegging ('at your scale', 'for a beginner'). Rescue blockquotes name plausibly-failable conditions (M5 run crashed/stalled in delivery), not redundant gates.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'eval' earned in body at line 46. Primitives named in canonical signposting sections (Big Idea, What You'll Learn, Key Concepts) are exempt per the rule-2 carve-out because they land operationally in this module's exercises/lectures (session-shaper authoring, eval mapping). 'M4'/'M5'/'M6' bare abbreviations are permitted (file at M6, well past M2).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Agents 101 default).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Agents 101 business-audience default. This is an AE101 file (software-engineer ICs); the SVP-marketing flinch test does not govern this audience. Tech terms are earned/landed per rule 2 instead.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'memory' used correctly as the persistent gap-home ('gaps belong in memory', 'rules that would have prevented the mistake upstream'). No 'context compounds' misuse; the two terms are not blurred.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Two rescue/accept-loss blockquotes (lines 27, 29) are recovery branches, normally banned in body, but the maintainer block at line 79 documents them as a deliberate exception (M5 run can crash mid-delivery; scoped rescue then a softer floor). Blockquote shape isolates them from the golden-path narrative. In-scope-by-design; not re-flagged.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: About prompt-block output shape (ask + destination + 'show me before saving'). This module file contains no fenced prompts; prompts live in the linked exercise files.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module. Compound-shape varies per module — never repeat same Debrief.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No Q1/Q2/Q3 retro shape. The compound move is explicitly compound-by-subtraction this module ('Cut one rule from ./CLAUDE.local.md the diagnosis killed', line 16), and the '## Human close' states no compound prompt fires here (encode already happened at skill ship). Distinct shape, not a repeated review-rewrite-report Debrief.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle another module's voice.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Voice holds the M6 practitioner-fluency mood throughout (forward, calm, kit-is-yours). No M1 joyful-creation or M4 unsettled-unease voice bleeding in.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't ask student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The two-run diff at Phase 1 is diagnostic reading (name gaps, route them), not error-catching of Claude's output. Self-critique-before-ship is routed through Claude ('ask Claude to audit its own work' shape, per the exercise's self-audit forcing function), not a manual student spot-check.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Simulation personas never alone.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: About self-study simulation defaults (Teacher Claude / Nerd present). No simulation persona surface in this module file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No "What research says" callout boxes
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No 'What research says' / 'studies show' callout boxes. Klaassen pre-read is a one-line inline citation (line 8), exactly the prescribed default.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student moves stay in conversation with agent.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Student moves named in body stay in conversation ('nudge it once', 'paste the re-send', 'ask it to surface'). The rule cut at './CLAUDE.local.md' (line 16) is a diagnosis-driven edit landed in the exercise's conversation flow, not a mechanic-mode editor task in the module body. No 'do it manually' forcing function.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 16 names the layer explicitly: './CLAUDE.local.md' (personal, gitignored). No unqualified 'CLAUDE.md' in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Zero em-dashes (and zero en-dashes) in the body region. Sentences are short and declarative. The hook auto-fix would have caught any survivor; none present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: '## Start here' leads with a verb. Canonical architecture sections ('## Big Idea', '## What You'll Learn', '## Key Concepts', '## Next') are exempt per the rule-17 carve-out. '## Human close' is a module-specific reflective closer (reader's job is reflection, not doing), so the declarative noun-phrase header is acceptable. LO bullets all lead with command verbs (Diagnose, Route, Cut, Surface, Author, Map, Encode, Generalize).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use target's title, never filename.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Every inline link uses title-style text ('[The 2 frontiers]', '[Story of Module 6]', '[How the best do CI/CD at agent scale]', '[My AI Had Already Fixed the Code Before I Saw It]'). No bare-filename link text and no code-span backtick paths in prose.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: By M6 the collision-words (memory, skill, rule) are long-disambiguated upstream; this module file authors to specific named targets ('./CLAUDE.local.md', '~/.claude/skills/' in maintainer/Artefact rows) rather than introducing an ambiguous first-authored write in body. The 'skill' usage is consistently the personal-kit skill the student authors. No fresh collision left undisambiguated.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body counts: Claude 3, agent 2, LLM 1 — no assistant-shaped 10:0 skew. Uses are contextually correct: 'LLM-based' for the thinking/judging topic (line 46), 'agent-produced'/'agent scale' for produced-thing and acting, 'Claude' for product/invocation. Default-to-specific honored.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No tomorrow/overnight/this morning/tonight/yesterday in body. Session-relative and module-relative phrasing used instead ('while the room reads', 'The next task you start', 'when this loop has to scale past you').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in flow.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No self-aware pedagogy headers ('Meta-moment', 'Step back', 'Moment of truth') and no contemplative-pause sentence prefixes ('sit with', 'take a moment', 'let it land', 'breathe'). '## Human close' is a structural closer section, not a reflection-announcing label; its body carries the reflection through the move itself ('If you can name the move in one sentence, that's the marker').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No meta-ask when student has already decided.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: About prompt-level meta-asks ('Ask me which one') after the student already decided. The module file carries no fenced prompts; this surface lives in the linked exercise files. No meta-ask in module body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: `.md` is default file type for student artifacts across curriculum.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No .txt or .docx in body. Artifact references are '.md'-shaped or path-based (./CLAUDE.local.md, ~/.claude/skills/<skill>/SKILL.md in maintainer rows). No save-prompt in body that defaults to a wrong extension.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cut NVA: true but not moving student forward.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body is tight; each beat advances the diagnose-route-author-encode arc. Rescue blockquotes carry only the decision the student needs (wait / stop / accept partial), no leaked mechanism. No administrative aside or implementation-detail-before-use in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body "read it" / "look at it" / "notice X" after chat-print = dead weight.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'while the room reads' (line 27) describes the room reading the catching-up run during a wait window, not a 'read it' command after a chat-print. No 'Look at it' / 'Study the table' / 'Notice the row' patronizing narration in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forward-callout closer: student as grammatical subject.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The going-deeper callout (line 68) uses the rule's own canonical good fix-shape verbatim: 'when this loop has to scale past you' — student framed forward from their own arc, no role-gating to a CTO/staff-engineer they don't inhabit. '## Next' closes 'It's yours now' with student as subject.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Operational reminders live at moment of use, not only where introduced.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Worktree-path convention and session-open operational state are restated where the student acts: '## Start here' and the maintainer Delivery-architecture note name the M5 worktree at '../<repo>-m5'. Per-module operational restatement is present rather than DRY'd out to prework only.
